title: AASRA Driver Dispatch — Driver Assigned Bookings Inbox (Frontend)

requirements.summary: Build a driver dispatch inbox UI to view assigned bookings, inspect details, and take status actions with validation.
acceptanceCriteria: A driver can navigate to a dedicated dispatch screen and see only their assigned bookings.
Selecting a booking shows full details and available actions appropriate to its current status.
Refuse/Cancel actions require a non-empty reason before submission and show inline validation errors if missing.
After any status mutation, the UI refreshes using React Query invalidation so the list and details reflect the updated status.

file_operations.description: Create the /driver/dispatch screen: authenticated driver-only inbox with an “Assigned Bookings” list (newest first from backend), a selectable detail panel, and actions (Accept, Refuse w/ required reason, Mark Completed, Cancel w/ required reason). Use the authorization component patterns to require Internet Identity authentication and to handle authorization traps gracefully in UI messaging. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Implement inline validation using the existing FieldError component for reason-required actions and disable/guard actions based on current booking status (e.g., pending -> accept/refuse; accepted -> complete/cancel; completed/cancelled/refused -> no mutations).

requirements.summary: Add routing and navigation entry points for the driver dispatch workflow and quick jump to location update.
acceptanceCriteria: A new route exists for a driver dispatch inbox and is registered in the TanStack Router route tree.
From the dispatch booking details, the driver can navigate to the existing location update screen for that booking id.
The Profile page shows an “Open Dispatch” (or similar) button/section only when the caller has a driver profile.

file_operations.description: Register a new TanStack Router route for /driver/dispatch and wire it to the new DriverDispatchPage so it is reachable via navigation.

Add a driver-only “Open Dispatch” entry point that appears only when the caller has a driver profile (based on useGetCallerUserProfile). Use the authorization component patterns already used in the app (authenticated-only screens) to avoid showing dispatch entry for guests/customers. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Wire a prominent “Update Location” navigation action in the booking details panel that routes to the existing /driver/track/$bookingId page for the selected booking.

requirements.summary: Add React Query hooks for driver dispatch bookings and driver status updates with consistent keys and invalidation.
acceptanceCriteria: Frontend has dedicated hooks for: (1) get assigned bookings for caller, (2) driver update booking status with optional reason.
On mutation success, relevant queries are invalidated (assigned bookings list, and any booking detail/tracking queries for the affected booking).
Hooks gracefully handle missing actor/identity similar to existing hooks (no crashes).

file_operations.description: Add React Query hooks: useGetDriverDispatchBookings() (calls actor.getDriverDispatchBookings) and useUpdateDriverBookingStatus() (calls actor.updateDriverBookingStatus). Match existing hook patterns (actor dependency checks, enabled flags) and use stable query keys (e.g., ['driverDispatch','bookings']).

On mutation success, invalidate: the dispatch list query key, existing per-booking keys (e.g., ['booking', bookingId]) and tracking keys used elsewhere (e.g., ['tracking','booking', bookingId] and ['tracking','location', bookingId]) so UI refreshes consistently after actions.

Adopt the new driver dispatch hooks for data loading and actions; ensure the page does not crash when actor/identity is missing and instead shows the existing AuthRequiredScreen (authorization component patterns). Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Apply a cohesive warm/amber, mobile-first driver dispatch theme with clear status hierarchy and prominent actions.
acceptanceCriteria: Dispatch UI is readable and usable on narrow mobile screens without horizontal scrolling.
Statuses are visually distinct (e.g., Pending/Accepted/Completed/Cancelled/Refused) using consistent badge styles.
Primary actions (Accept/Refuse/Complete/Cancel/Update Location) are visually prominent and not easily mis-tapped.

file_operations.description: Implement a mobile-first layout (stacked list + details on small screens; split-pane on larger screens) using existing Tailwind + shadcn/ui components (Card, Button, Badge, Alert, Skeleton). Ensure touch-friendly button sizing and spacing for Android PWA usage.

Add consistent, high-contrast status badges and grouping (e.g., Pending/Accepted as active; Completed; Cancelled/Refused) using existing Badge variants and warm palette classes consistent with current app styling (as seen on TrackingPage).

If needed to support the dispatch screen’s readability on narrow viewports, add minimal global utility adjustments consistent with existing mobile-friendly rules (avoid new stylesheet files). Keep changes limited and aligned with the existing warm/amber theme.
